title: Exostema angustifolium
taxonomy: Exostema angustifolium is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. As a member of the Rubiaceae family, Exostema angustifolium is closely related to other plants such as gardenias and jasmine. The plant's classification is further defined by its genus, Exostema, which is a group of flowering plants primarily found in the tropical and subtropical regions. Exostema angustifolium is often referred to as a member of the family Rubiaceae, which is one of the largest and most diverse groups of flowering plants.

intro: Exostema angustifolium, also known as the "wild coffee" plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.
